reasoning: Short-term rates appear relatively oversold (dropped abruptly to 6.657 after hovering near 6.8-7.0 most of mid-2025), so a mean-reverting bounce over the next week is likelier than not (p=0.59). Over the 6-week to 6-month horizons, both the trailing linear trend (-0.007/day) and broader macroeconomic forecasts (with analysts projecting rates near 6% by late 2025) dictate structural decreases in rates. Taking this baseline negative drift into account, the expected value drops below 6.657, keeping the probabilities of an increase under 0.5. In accordance with the requirement that uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, the longer-term forecasts are stepped progressively closer to 0.5 (0.44 -> 0.47 -> 0.49), balancing the negative drift with the massively expanding standard deviation across 3 to 6 months.
